title: teucrium rotundifolium subsp. ruiziorum
intro: Teucrium rotundifolium subsp. ruiziorum, also known as Cuban germander, is a plant species within the family Lamiaceae, characterized by its round leaves and woody stems. The plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, due to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Cuban germander include its potential to protect against liver damage, reduce fever, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. It is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping the leaves in hot water, and can also be found in tincture or essential oil form, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
